workflow: RupeeKit Workflow 8C - Anti-Repetition AI Social Creative Generator

creativePack.hook: Myth: Your CTC is your take-home pay. Fact: Deductions matter more than you think!

facebook.caption: It's a common misconception: many believe their Cost-to-Company (CTC) is the amount they'll actually receive. But the reality of your 'in-hand' salary is often quite different! From Provident Fund (PF) to professional tax and income tax, various deductions play a significant role in shaping your monthly take-home pay. Understanding these differences is key for smart financial planning. Don't get surprised on payday. Use RupeeKit's Salary In-Hand Calculator to get an educational estimate of your actual earnings and compare the impact of different deductions. Plan better for your financial future. Find it here: https://www.rupeekit.co.in/tools/salary-in-hand-calculator-india

instagram.caption: Myth: Your CTC is your take-home pay. Fact: Deductions matter more than you think! 🤯 Many of us equate our CTC with what we'll actually receive, but that's a common mistake. Your in-hand salary is what hits your bank account after essential deductions like PF, professional tax, and income tax. Knowing this difference is crucial for budgeting and financial planning. Get a clear educational estimate of your monthly take-home pay with RupeeKit's Salary In-Hand Calculator. Understand your numbers and plan confidently. Ready to compare your inputs? Visit: https://www.rupeekit.co.in/tools/salary-in-hand-calculator-india

facebook.prompt: A clean, modern comparison card design with a white background. On the left side, a section titled 'Myth: CTC = Take-Home Pay' in deep blue, with a subtle graphic representing a large sum (CTC) being visually reduced by a red 'X' or a 'minus' symbol. On the right side, a section titled 'Fact: Deductions Matter' in fresh green, with a graphic showing a smaller, but clearly defined, amount (In-Hand Salary) emerging from a calculation process, perhaps with small icons representing PF, Tax, etc. The overall aesthetic is professional, finance utility brand look. No human faces, no personal identity. Ensure the top-right corner is clear of any design elements or text. Text on card: 'CTC vs. In-Hand: Know the Difference'. Brand context: RupeeKit, an India-focused finance calculator utility brand using deep blue and fresh green accents. Final output should be a sharp, social-media-ready PNG or JPG image under 10 MB.

instagram.prompt: A square, visually balanced comparison card. The background is light grey or white. A prominent vertical line or subtle divider splits the image. On the left, a deep blue panel or section with the text 'Myth: CTC is your Take-Home Pay' in a clean, bold font. Below it, a simple, abstract graphic implying a large initial amount. On the right, a fresh green panel or section with the text 'Fact: Deductions Impact In-Hand Salary' in a similar font. Below it, an abstract graphic showing a refined, smaller amount with subtle calculator elements. The design is minimalist, clear, and professional, avoiding any human elements. Top-right corner must be empty. Text on card: 'CTC ≠ In-Hand Pay'. Brand context: RupeeKit, an India-focused finance calculator utility brand using deep blue and fresh green accents. No humans, no human faces, no people, no personal identity, no mascot. Final output should be a sharp, social-media-ready PNG or JPG image under 10 MB.

pinterest.prompt: A tall, elegant comparison card for Pinterest. The design features a clean white background. A large, readable header at the top (not in the top-right corner) saying 'CTC vs. In-Hand Salary: The Real Difference'. Below this, two distinct vertical sections or columns. The left column, with deep blue accents, illustrates 'Myth: CTC is Final' with a graphic of a large, unchecked number. The right column, with fresh green accents, illustrates 'Fact: Deductions Are Key' with a graphic showing a calculation process leading to a smaller, clear number representing in-hand pay. Use subtle, abstract finance-related icons or shapes. No human figures or faces. The top-right corner is kept completely clear for logo overlay. Text on card: 'Understand Your Actual Pay'. Brand context: RupeeKit, an India-focused finance calculator utility brand using deep blue and fresh green accents. Final output should be a sharp, social-media-ready PNG or JPG image under 10 MB.

creativePack.negativePrompt: avoid humans, faces, personal identity, clutter, misleading claims, bank/loan ad look, watermark, tiny unreadable text, fake RupeeKit logo, fake RupeeKit wordmark, fake website URL, important elements in top-right corner, dark or busy backgrounds

comfyUiNotes.notes: Simple composition, focus on clear text and color contrast for comparison. Use a clean, sans-serif font for any text on the image.

safetyNotes: Ensure no implied guarantees or 'advice'. Stick to 'estimate' and 'understand'. Verify no forbidden phrases appear in captions or image text.
